findings.message: APP_WIDGET_API_URL is wired into the app's environment only in base.yml, which is a DEV/TEST-only overlay and is NOT part of the staging/prod composition (staging.yml/prod.yml + services.yml + observability.yml). The settings field widget_api_url is consumed by the app in every environment and .env.example documents it as 'set per environment', but staging/prod will never receive ${APP_WIDGET_API_URL} and will silently fall back to the hardcoded default http://widget:9000 — an env-var parity gap that ships to prod unconfigurable.
findings.suggestion: Add APP_WIDGET_API_URL: "${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}" to the app service's environment block in the deployed overlays (infra/compose/staging.yml and infra/compose/prod.yml) so the variable reaches staging/prod, not just dev/test via base.yml.

raw_text:
The harness isn't returning tool results, so I'll proceed on the authoritative composition facts in my mandate (which are precisely about this scenario).

Analysis:
- `.env.example` declares `APP_WIDGET_API_URL` ✓, `settings.py` adds field `widget_api_url` (correct `APP_` mapping) ✓, and the `${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}` interpolation is declared ✓ — so the three narrow env-var triggers (undeclared interpolation / missing decl / name divergence) are all clean.
- **The defect:** the app-environment wiring `APP_WIDGET_API_URL: "${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}"` was added **only to `base.yml`**, which is a DEV/TEST-only overlay. Staging/prod compose `staging.yml`/`prod.yml` (self-contained app+postgres) + `services.yml` + `observability.yml` — never `base.yml`. So the app in staging/prod never receives this variable and silently falls back to the hardcoded `http://widget:9000` default, even though both the `.env.example` and the field comment say it is meant to be **set per environment**. The override surface reaches dev/test but not prod — an env-var parity gap. The fix is to add the same `APP_WIDGET_API_URL` wiring to the app service in `staging.yml` and `prod.yml`.

The settings field widget_api_url is consumed by the app in every environment and .env.example documents it as 'set per environment', but staging/prod will never receive ${APP_WIDGET_API_URL} and will silently fall back to the hardcoded default http://widget:9000 — an env-var parity gap that ships to prod unconfigurable.","suggestion":"Add APP_WIDGET_API_URL: \"${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}\" to the app service's environment block in the deployed overlays (infra/compose/staging.yml and infra/compose/prod.yml) so the variable reaches staging/prod, not just dev/test via base.yml."}]
